heading: Intercity and commuter rail actions considered discriminatory
label: 12162 Intercity and commuter rail actions considered discriminatory

children.heading: One car per train rule
children.label: 1 One car per train rule
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person who provides intercity rail transportation to fail to have at least one passenger car per train that is readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, in accordance with regulations issued under , as soon as practicable, but in no event later than 5 years after .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this titleJuly 26, 1990

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection with respect to individuals who use wheelchairs, it shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person to purchase or lease any new rail passenger cars for use in intercity rail transportation, and for which a solicitation is made later than 30 days after , unless all such rail cars are readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation in regulations issued under .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this titleJuly 26, 1990

children.heading: Special rule for single-level passenger coaches for individuals who use wheelchairs
children.label: B Special rule for single-level passenger coaches for individuals who use wheelchairs
i
children.text: be able to be entered by an individual who uses a wheelchair;

children.label: ii
children.text: have space to park and secure a wheelchair;

children.label: iii
children.text: have a seat to which a passenger in a wheelchair can transfer, and a space to fold and store such passenger’s wheelchair; and

children.label: iv
children.text: have a restroom usable by an individual who uses a wheelchair,

children.heading: Special rule for single-level dining cars for individuals who use wheelchairs
children.label: C Special rule for single-level dining cars for individuals who use wheelchairs
i
children.text: be able to be entered from the station platform by an individual who uses a wheelchair; or

children.label: ii
children.text: have a restroom usable by an individual who uses a wheelchair if no restroom is provided in such car for any passenger.

children.heading: Special rule for bi-level dining cars for individuals who use wheelchairs
children.label: D Special rule for bi-level dining cars for individuals who use wheelchairs
i
children.text: be able to be entered by an individual who uses a wheelchair;

children.label: ii
children.text: have space to park and secure a wheelchair;

children.label: iii
children.text: have a seat to which a passenger in a wheelchair can transfer, or a space to fold and store such passenger’s wheelchair; or

children.label: iv
children.text: have a restroom usable by an individual who uses a wheelchair.

children.label: I
children.text: to park and secure wheelchairs (to accommodate individuals who wish to remain in their wheelchairs) equal to not less than one-half of the number of single-level rail passenger coaches in such train; and

children.label: II
children.text: to fold and store wheelchairs (to accommodate individuals who wish to transfer to coach seats) equal to not less than one-half of the number of single-level rail passenger coaches in such train,

children.label: I
children.text: to park and secure wheelchairs (to accommodate individuals who wish to remain in their wheelchairs) equal to not less than the total number of single-level rail passenger coaches in such train; and

children.label: II
children.text: to fold and store wheelchairs (to accommodate individuals who wish to transfer to coach seats) equal to not less than the total number of single-level rail passenger coaches in such train,

children.heading: Location
children.label: B Location
children.text: Spaces required by subparagraph (A) shall be located in single-level rail passenger coaches or food service cars.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: Of the number of spaces required on a train by subparagraph (A), not more than two spaces to park and secure wheelchairs nor more than two spaces to fold and store wheelchairs shall be located in any one coach or food service car.

children.heading: Other accessibility features
children.label: D Other accessibility features
children.text: Single-level rail passenger coaches and food service cars on which the spaces required by subparagraph (A) are located shall have a restroom usable by an individual who uses a wheelchair and shall be able to be entered from the station platform by an individual who uses a wheelchair.

children.label: I
children.text: the car adjacent to the end of the dining car through which a wheelchair may enter is itself accessible to a wheelchair;

children.label: II
children.text: such passenger can exit to the platform from the car such passenger occupies, move down the platform, and enter the adjacent accessible car described in subclause (I) without the necessity of the train being moved within the station; and

children.label: III
children.text: space to park and secure a wheelchair is available in the dining car at the time such passenger wishes to eat (if such passenger wishes to remain in a wheelchair), or space to store and fold a wheelchair is available in the dining car at the time such passenger wishes to eat (if such passenger wishes to transfer to a dining car seat); and

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate auxiliary aids and services, including a hard surface on which to eat, shall be provided to ensure that other equivalent food service is available to individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, and to passengers traveling with such individuals.

children.label: i
children.text: if such train includes a bi-level lounge car purchased after , table service in such lounge car shall be provided to individuals who use wheelchairs and to other passengers; andJuly 26, 1990

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate auxiliary aids and services, including a hard surface on which to eat, shall be provided to ensure that other equivalent food service is available to individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, and to passengers traveling with such individuals.

children.heading: One car per train rule
children.label: 1 One car per train rule
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person who provides commuter rail transportation to fail to have at least one passenger car per train that is readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, in accordance with regulations issued under , as soon as practicable, but in no event later than 5 years after .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this titleJuly 26, 1990

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person to purchase or lease any new rail passenger cars for use in commuter rail transportation, and for which a solicitation is made later than 30 days after , unless all such rail cars are readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation in regulations issued under .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this titleJuly 26, 1990

children.label: i
children.text: a restroom usable by an individual who uses a wheelchair if no restroom is provided in such car for any passenger;

children.label: ii
children.text: space to fold and store a wheelchair; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a seat to which a passenger who uses a wheelchair can transfer.

children.heading: Used rail cars
children.label: c Used rail cars
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person to purchase or lease a used rail passenger car for use in intercity or commuter rail transportation, unless such person makes demonstrated good faith efforts to purchase or lease a used rail car that is readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation in regulations issued under .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this title

children.heading: Remanufacturing
children.label: 1 Remanufacturing
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person to remanufacture a rail passenger car for use in intercity or commuter rail transportation so as to extend its usable life for 10 years or more, unless the rail car, to the maximum extent feasible, is made readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation in regulations issued under .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this title

children.heading: Purchase or lease
children.label: 2 Purchase or lease
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person to purchase or lease a remanufactured rail passenger car for use in intercity or commuter rail transportation unless such car was remanufactured in accordance with paragraph (1).section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29

children.heading: New stations
children.label: 1 New stations
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a person to build a new station for use in intercity or commuter rail transportation that is not readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation in regulations issued under .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this title

children.heading: Failure to make readily accessible
children.label: A Failure to make readily accessible

children.heading: General rule
children.label: i General rule
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for a responsible person to fail to make existing stations in the intercity rail transportation system, and existing key stations in commuter rail transportation systems, readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as prescribed by the Secretary of Transportation in regulations issued under .section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29section 12164 of this title

children.heading: Intercity rail
children.label: I Intercity rail
children.text: All stations in the intercity rail transportation system shall be made readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as soon as practicable, but in no event later than 20 years after .July 26, 1990

children.heading: Commuter rail
children.label: II Commuter rail
children.text: Key stations in commuter rail transportation systems shall be made readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, as soon as practicable but in no event later than 3 years after , except that the time limit may be extended by the Secretary of Transportation up to 20 years after , in a case where the raising of the entire passenger platform is the only means available of attaining accessibility or where other extraordinarily expensive structural changes are necessary to attain accessibility.July 26, 1990July 26, 1990

children.heading: Designation of key stations
children.label: iii Designation of key stations
children.text: Each commuter authority shall designate the key stations in its commuter rail transportation system, in consultation with individuals with disabilities and organizations representing such individuals, taking into consideration such factors as high ridership and whether such station serves as a transfer or feeder station. Before the final designation of key stations under this clause, a commuter authority shall hold a public hearing.

children.heading: Plans and milestones
children.label: iv Plans and milestones
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall require the appropriate person to develop a plan for carrying out this subparagraph that reflects consultation with individuals with disabilities affected by such plan and that establishes milestones for achievement of the requirements of this subparagraph.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: i General rule
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination, for purposes of and , with respect to alterations of an existing station or part thereof in the intercity or commuter rail transportation systems that affect or could affect the usability of the station or part thereof, for the responsible person, owner, or person in control of the station to fail to make the alterations in such a manner that, to the maximum extent feasible, the altered portions of the station are readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, upon completion of such alterations.section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29

children.heading: Alterations to a primary function area
children.label: ii Alterations to a primary function area
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination, for purposes of and , with respect to alterations that affect or could affect the usability of or access to an area of the station containing a primary function, for the responsible person, owner, or person in control of the station to fail to make the alterations in such a manner that, to the maximum extent feasible, the path of travel to the altered area, and the bathrooms, telephones, and drinking fountains serving the altered area, are readily accessible to and usable by individuals with disabilities, including individuals who use wheelchairs, upon completion of such alterations, where such alterations to the path of travel or the bathrooms, telephones, and drinking fountains serving the altered area are not disproportionate to the overall alterations in terms of cost and scope (as determined under criteria established by the Attorney General).section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29

children.heading: Required cooperation
children.label: C Required cooperation
children.text: It shall be considered discrimination for purposes of and for an owner, or person in control, of a station governed by subparagraph (A) or (B) to fail to provide reasonable cooperation to a responsible person with respect to such station in that responsible person’s efforts to comply with such subparagraph. An owner, or person in control, of a station shall be liable to a responsible person for any failure to provide reasonable cooperation as required by this subparagraph. Failure to receive reasonable cooperation required by this subparagraph shall not be a defense to a claim of discrimination under this chapter.section 12132 of this titlesection 794 of title 29
